enonce.texte:
Match the household chores with their correct definitions.

1. Sweep
2. Mop
3. Dust
4. Wash
5. Iron
6. Tidy up
7. Take out the trash
Definitions:
A. To clean a floor with a wet cloth or mop
B. To remove dust from surfaces
C. To clean with water and soap
D. To make things neat and orderly
E. To remove wrinkles from clothes
F. To remove rubbish from the house
G. To clean a floor with a broom

questions.texte: Match each chore with the correct definition by writing the correct letter (A-G) in your notebook.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct words from the box: broom, dustpan, mop, sponge, vacuum cleaner, detergent, iron

questions.texte: I use a ______ to sweep the floor.
questions.reponse: broom

questions.texte: After sweeping, I use a ______ to collect the dust.
questions.reponse: dustpan

questions.texte: To clean the tiles, I use a ______ with soap and water.
questions.reponse: mop

questions.texte: I need a ______ to wash the dishes.
questions.reponse: sponge

questions.texte: My mother uses a ______ to clean the carpets.
questions.reponse: vacuum cleaner

questions.texte: She uses ______ to remove stains from clothes.
questions.reponse: detergent

questions.texte: My father uses an ______ to press his shirts.
questions.reponse: iron

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the simple past tense of the verbs in brackets.

questions.texte: My mother ______ (cook) dinner yesterday evening.
questions.reponse: cooked

questions.texte: I ______ (sweep) the floor after school.
questions.reponse: swept

questions.texte: My sister ______ (wash) the dishes last night.
questions.reponse: washed

questions.texte: We ______ (clean) the house on Saturday.
questions.reponse: cleaned

questions.texte: My father ______ (take out) the trash this morning.
questions.reponse: took out

questions.texte: She ______ (iron) her school uniform yesterday.
questions.reponse: ironed

intitule: Reading Comprehension: A Weekly Chore Schedule

enonce.texte:
Read the chore schedule and answer the questions.

📋 OUR WEEKLY CHORE SCHEDULE

In our family, everyone has chores to do. Here is our schedule:

  • Monday: Father sweeps the living room. Mother washes the clothes. I take out the trash.
  • Tuesday: Mother mops the kitchen floor. Sister dusts the furniture. I clean my bedroom.
  • Wednesday: Father waters the plants. Mother cooks dinner. Sister washes the dishes. I do my homework.
  • Thursday: Mother irons the clothes. Sister sweeps the backyard. I organize my school bag.
  • Friday: Father cleans the car. Mother does the shopping. Sister mops the bathroom. I take out the trash.

We all work together to keep our home clean and organized!

questions.texte: What does the mother do on Monday?
questions.reponse: On Monday, the mother washes the clothes.

questions.texte: Identify three chores that the sister does during the week.
questions.reponse: Three chores the sister does are: dusts the furniture (Tuesday), washes the dishes (Wednesday), sweeps the backyard (Thursday), mops the bathroom (Friday). (Any three correct answers).

questions.texte: What chore does the father do on Friday?
questions.reponse: On Friday, the father cleans the car.

questions.texte: What does the student do on Wednesday?
questions.reponse: On Wednesday, the student does his homework.

questions.texte: Why is it important to have a chore schedule in the family?
questions.reponse: A chore schedule is important because it helps to divide work fairly among family members. It ensures that all chores are done regularly and that the home stays clean and organized. It also teaches responsibility and teamwork.

enonce.texte: Rewrite the sentences in the negative form using the simple past tense.

questions.texte: I washed the dishes yesterday.
questions.reponse: I did not wash the dishes yesterday.

questions.texte: She swept the floor this morning.
questions.reponse: She did not sweep the floor this morning.

questions.texte: They cleaned the kitchen on Saturday.
questions.reponse: They did not clean the kitchen on Saturday.

questions.texte: My father took out the trash yesterday.
questions.reponse: My father did not take out the trash yesterday.

questions.texte: We ironed our clothes last night.
questions.reponse: We did not iron our clothes last night.

enonce.texte: Write questions in the simple past tense for the following sentences.

questions.texte: She cooked dinner last night.
questions.reponse: Did she cook dinner last night?

questions.texte: They swept the yard yesterday.
questions.reponse: Did they sweep the yard yesterday?

questions.texte: He washed the car on Sunday.
questions.reponse: Did he wash the car on Sunday?

questions.texte: We cleaned the bathroom this morning.
questions.reponse: Did you clean the bathroom this morning?

questions.texte: She took out the trash after dinner.
questions.reponse: Did she take out the trash after dinner?

enonce.texte:
Read the text and answer the questions.

Planning Household Chores

Planning household chores is important for every family. Last week, my mother created a new chore chart for our family. She divided the chores among my father, my sister, and me. My father was responsible for taking out the trash and cleaning the garden. My sister had to dust the furniture and sweep the living room. My job was to wash the dishes and tidy up my bedroom.

On Monday, I washed the dishes after dinner. On Tuesday, my sister dusted the furniture. On Wednesday, my father watered the plants in the garden. On Thursday, I organized my bedroom. On Friday, my sister swept the living room. On Saturday, my father cleaned the garden. On Sunday, my mother cooked a special meal.

I learned that planning chores makes everything easier. Everyone knows what to do, and the house stays clean.

questions.texte: Who created the chore chart in the family?
questions.reponse: The mother created the chore chart.

questions.texte: What chores did the father have to do?
questions.reponse: The father had to take out the trash and clean the garden.

questions.texte: What did the student do on Monday and Thursday?
questions.reponse: On Monday, the student washed the dishes. On Thursday, the student organized the bedroom.

questions.texte: What did the sister do on Tuesday and Friday?
questions.reponse: On Tuesday, the sister dusted the furniture. On Friday, the sister swept the living room.

questions.texte: Why does planning chores make things easier according to the text?
questions.reponse: Planning chores makes things easier because everyone knows what to do. This avoids confusion and ensures that the house stays clean. It also helps to share work fairly among family members.

enonce.texte:
Write a chore schedule for your family for one week. Write about 100-120 words.

Include:

  • What chores each family member does each day
  • Use the simple past tense
  • Write in paragraph form

questions.texte: Write a chore schedule for your family.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

Last week, we planned our family chores carefully. On Monday, I swept the living room while my sister mopped the kitchen floor. My mother washed the clothes. On Tuesday, my father cleaned the car and I took out the trash. My sister dusted the furniture. On Wednesday, I washed the dishes after dinner. My sister organized the bookshelves. My father watered the garden. On Thursday, I cleaned my bedroom and my sister ironed the clothes. My mother cooked dinner. On Friday, my father took out the trash and I mopped the bathroom. My sister swept the yard. On Saturday, we all cleaned the house together. On Sunday, we rested. Planning chores helped us to work as a team.

intitule: Grammar: Simple Past Tense - Mixed Forms

enonce.texte:
Complete the paragraph with the simple past tense of the verbs in brackets.

Yesterday, I (1) ______ (wake up) at 6:00 AM. My mother (2) ______ (already / prepare) breakfast. She (3) ______ (cook) rice and beans. I (4) ______ (eat) quickly and then I (5) ______ (sweep) the floor. My sister (6) ______ (wash) the dishes. My father (7) ______ (take out) the trash. We (8) ______ (not / watch) television because we were too busy. At 7:30 AM, we (9) ______ (leave) for school. We (10) ______ (have) a good day.

questions.texte: Complete the paragraph with the correct past tense forms.
questions.reponse: 1. woke up, 2. had already prepared, 3. cooked, 4. ate, 5. swept, 6. washed, 7. took out, 8. did not watch, 9. left, 10. had

intitule: Writing: Daily Routine in the Past

enonce.texte:
Write a paragraph (about 80-100 words) describing what you did yesterday to help with household chores.

Use the simple past tense and include at least five different chores.

questions.texte: Write a paragraph describing what you did yesterday.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

Yesterday was a busy day. I woke up at 6:00 AM and helped my mother with household chores. First, I swept the living room and the kitchen. Then, I mopped the kitchen floor with soap and water. After that, I washed the dishes from breakfast. My sister dusted the furniture and cleaned the windows. I also took out the trash and organized my bedroom. In the afternoon, I ironed my school uniform for today. My father cleaned the garden and watered the plants. At night, we all watched television together. I felt happy because I helped my family.

intitule: Reading Comprehension: A Letter About Chores

enonce.texte:
Read the letter and answer the questions.

Dear Auntie Marie,

I hope you are well. I am writing to tell you about my new chore schedule. Last week, my mother made a new plan for household chores. Every day, I have to do my homework before I can watch television. My sister and I take turns washing the dishes. She washes them on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday. I wash them on Tuesday, Thursday, and Saturday. On Sunday, we both wash them together.

My father always takes out the trash. My mother cooks dinner. I think we work well as a team. Last Sunday, I helped my mother cook a special meal. I peeled the vegetables and she fried the fish. It was delicious! I also cleaned my bedroom on Saturday. I swept the floor and organized my books.

I have learned that everyone must contribute to keep the house clean. Please come and visit us soon.

Your loving nephew,
Fils

questions.texte: Who is Fils writing to?
questions.reponse: Fils is writing to his Auntie Marie.

questions.texte: On which days does Fils wash the dishes?
questions.reponse: Fils washes the dishes on Tuesday, Thursday, and Saturday.

questions.texte: What did Fils do to help cook the special meal?
questions.reponse: Fils peeled the vegetables to help cook the special meal.

questions.texte: What did Fils learn about household chores?
questions.reponse: Fils learned that everyone must contribute to keep the house clean.

questions.texte: Based on the letter, do you think Fils is responsible? Justify your answer.
questions.reponse: Yes, Fils is very responsible. He follows the chore schedule, helps with cooking, cleans his bedroom, and seems to understand the importance of contributing to the household. He also works well with his sister, taking turns washing the dishes.

intitule: Grammar: Simple Past Tense - Verb Conjugation

enonce.texte:
Write the simple past tense of these verbs in the correct column (regular or irregular).

Verbs: cook, sweep, wash, clean, take, iron, dust, mop, put, make, do, go, have, see

questions.texte: Classify the verbs as regular or irregular and write their past tense forms.
questions.reponse:
Regular verbs: cook - cooked, wash - washed, clean - cleaned, iron - ironed, dust - dusted, mop - mopped

Irregular verbs: sweep - swept, take - took, put - put, make - made, do - did, go - went, have - had, see - saw

intitule: Writing: A Letter About Household Chores

enonce.texte:
Write a letter (about 100-120 words) to your pen pal telling them about how your family plans household chores.

Include:

  • Who does what chores
  • What you did last week (use simple past tense)
  • What you think about doing chores

questions.texte: Write a letter to your pen pal about household chores.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

Your Address
Yaoundé, Cameroon
15th March 2025

Dear Paul,

How are you? I hope you are well. I am writing to tell you about household chores in my family. My mother makes a schedule every week. My father takes out the trash and cleans the garden. My sister sweeps the floor and dusts the furniture. I wash the dishes and organize my bedroom.

Last week, I swept the living room on Monday. On Tuesday, I mopped the kitchen floor. On Wednesday, I washed the dishes after dinner. On Thursday, I cleaned my bedroom. On Friday, I helped my mother cook dinner. On Saturday, we all cleaned the house together. On Sunday, we rested.

I think doing chores is important. It teaches us responsibility. Please write back and tell me about your family.

Your friend,
Fils
